programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan puts on his thinking cap to investigate a murder at a benefactors' ball being chaired by Jesse. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's detective work results in the execution of a suspected serial bomber, but as the blasts continue he fears he's made a fatal mistake. Feature-length special of the crime drama, starring Dick and Barry Van Dyke, with Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble witnesses an accidental killing and tries to save an innocent man from being charged with murder.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A divorced woman asks Perry to defend her ex-husband, who is accused of murdering her son, but things are made complicated when she disappears.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The rangers turn to the Moscow police for assistance after a spate of warehouse robberies are linked to a Russian mobster

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The young daughter of a wealthy family finds her nanny dead, setting in motion an investigation that sheds an unflattering light on the lives of the rich and famous. Horatio and the team suspect the victim may have been having an affair with her employer, giving him a motive for murder, but eventually follow a trail of evidence to another suspect

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes involved in a personal problem concerning his boss and boss's wife while working on a construction project.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Wearing a bathrobe and sporting a black eye, beautiful Diana Reynolds enters Perry's office and asks him to defend her after she's accused of murder.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker gets trapped down a drain hole when he attempts to rescue a young boy. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Kensi and Deeks go undercover as a married couple in a quiet suburb as part of an investigation into a Russian sleeper cell. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Eric Christian Olsen

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team investigates the deaths of three arms smugglers killed in a warehouse by an illegal electronic super-weapon, and a gunrunner interviewed by Eric is shot during his questioning. However, the detectives' chief suspect uses anti-terrorist legislation known as the Patriot Act in a bid to throw them off track. Drama, starring David Caruso

Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. However, when he comes face to face with the fugitive, he is tricked into answering a phone wired to a car bomb

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect

programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a billionaire philanthropist, who has been found dead in his study. programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean cops enter the high-pressure world of fine dining when a famous chef is found stabbed to death the morning after the opening of his new restaurant. It proves to be a tricky investigation as the body was stored in a freezer, so the time of death cannot be identified - and when it turns out he was quite a hot-head in the kitchen, all his staff come under suspicion. Away from the case, Dwayne meets his match in the shape of a troublesome schoolboy, Florence has one last lesson in love for the inspector and JP makes a life-changing decision. programme.desc: Rivalries emerge as Leela finds herself up against Honey in the election for the prestigious role of Met Federation representative, and a newcomer from the West End is offered promotion ahead of Suzie following a successful joint operation. Will and Dan fall out when competition for a place on a driving course prompts Will to play a prank which leads to his friend's disqualification

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol has harsh words for David, and Alan is in for a nasty shock when he tries to unravel a mystery. Meanwhile, Cindy and Gita consider an afternoon's diversion

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy and Gita have a fun day out at the swimming pool, but Ian's back in the dog house. Nellie finds that 3 is a crowd in the pub, and Bianca, Ricky and Natalie go out clubbing

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Hyper plucks up the courage to tell his mates he is gay, a revelation which leads to a rift with Joe, whose hidden prejudices come to the surface

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: David manages to persuade his family to come and live with him in Cardale, but his happiness is marred by the discovery that a boy he helped deliver 11 years ago has contracted a potentially fatal illness. Will and Kate are still finding it hard to come to terms with baby Emily's condition and consider moving further afield, while new receptionist Joanne reckons Andrew could be ripe for romance. Starring Simon Shepherd, Saskia Wickham, Gary Mavers, Adrian Lukis and Shelagh McLeod

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Alison decides she can manage on her own and rents a remote country cottage, but the discovery that there's an arsonist at large soon has Paul fearing for her safety. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tamzin Malleson and Tim Vincent

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: A property war breaks out in Chigwell as man-eating neighbour Dorien and Tracey's son Garth compete for the flat above Sharon's cafe. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Matthew Savage

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana set out to help the local community by taking on a scheming estate agent who has got her claws into the hapless Harvey. Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole star in the retirement home comedy

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Hans succeeds in vaulting over the perimeter fence at the POW camp, while Gruber and Helga try to rescue the others

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lieutenant Commander Willoughby takes an instant liking to Compo, Clegg and Foggy and offers them the chance to become extremely wealthy. Ron Moody guest stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An armoured security van is discovered at the bottom of a lake, and the team makes a link to a disappearance 17 years previously and an unsolved killing, prompting them to reopen the case. Sandra feels she is making progress after finding the murder weapon, but suffers a setback when a key witness is killed. Detective drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. There is little sympathy when child-killer James Wade dies in his cell, but Leo's encounter with dying ex-prison inspector Rachel Kruger compels him to investigate the notorious Redhill jail - only to come up against a wall of silence. Meanwhile, Harry meets Wade's sister Miriam and agrees to help find her brother's killer. Forensic crime drama, starring William Gaminara, Emilia Fox and Tom Ward, with Juliet Aubrey, Gillian Kearney and Jaye Griffiths

programme.title: Mamwlad
programme.desc: The story of Margaret Haig Thomas, one of Wales' most prominent suffragettes, the shy girl from Monmouthshire who became a militant campaigner for the vote for women. During a lifetime of adventure 'Lady Rhondda' survived the sinking of RMS Lusitania, was jailed for planting a chemical bomb in Newport, and went on hunger strike in support of her political views. She also became one of the most successful businesswomen of her time. Ffion Hague talks to Haig Thomas's biographer, Professor Angela V John, about her subject's life and how her work continues to inspire females today

programme.title: Pwy Geith y Gig?

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A mother and daughter suddenly find their tumble drier has set alight, and it all goes wrong for a top British surfer as he rides a wave

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall guide two teams scouring south-west London for bargains to take to auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to Strawberry Hill House in Twickenham, one of the finest examples of Gothic revival architecture in the UK

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A blood donor session sees some awkward encounters, while Ash annoys a customer. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. Also, cameras follow Border Force officers intercepting potentially lethal medicines and members of the National Crime Agency as they raid the homes of currency counterfeiters. Presented by Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks at mid-terrace houses in Stoke-on-Trent and Wolverhampton and Martin Roberts explores a large listed building in Rochester

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A group of hikers whose Dartmoor walk turned serious when one of them fell and landed on rocks below. Plus, footage of a couple and their baby as they fled from a hotel fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Tim Weeks and John Cameron help two teams find items to sell for a profit at auction at the Kent County Showground. Charlie Ross pays a visit to Leeds Castle

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kat's contact with Patch causes alarm. Kam starts his new school. Australian drama, starring Siria Kickett and Yazeed Daher

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a house hunting mission in the Yorkshire Dales with a pair of brothers, one of whom wants his £500,000 budget to furnish him with a country home in rural isolation. While in the area, Nicki joins the Dales Park Rangers and helps them on their everyday rounds as well, as learning about the growing popularity of geocaching with walkers

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Morecambe Bay, where they get to meet to meet the buyer's cherished horse, Dragon, before pitching potential artworks inspired by the animal

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Popular quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace are joined by Italian chef Aldo Zilli, who challenges the six remaining hopefuls to recreate one of his favourite recipes. After battling with unfamiliar ingredients and techniques, the next task sees them serving up a dish inspired by someone they love or admire - knowing that only three of them will be awarded places in the final

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2020 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Over the past few months, more than 20,000 care home residents and staff have died of Covid-19 across the country. Panorama follows the struggle to protect residents, filming in two different homes over several months. What it reveals is the dedication of staff, the frustration of managers and heartache as more lives are lost. Alison Holt asks if care homes were abandoned to fight the virus alone

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Kate Prince is a remarkable British dance pioneer whose renowned ZooNation company has transformed the profile of hip-hop and street dance across the country. Alan Yentob meets the choreographer as she embarks on her latest project, Message in a Bottle, a West End production set to the songs of Sting. The programme follows the highs and lows of birthing a complex new dance show from day one in the studio to opening night, while also exploring the wider impact of Prince's work and politics on a younger generation of upcoming dancers

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The freak wave that capsized the boat of two young men attempting to set a world record rowing the Atlantic. Plus, the family day out in the snow that almost ended in tragedy when a man suffered an accident while sledging

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups come under threat from members of a rival meerkat group, Tosca gives birth and Shakespeare continues to fight for his life after being bitten by a snake

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's next leg begins in Ho Chi Minh City, where he uncovers the traumatic 20th-century history of Vietnam, a country that went from a French colony to a socialist republic. He also braves the city streets on a motorcycle, samples French-Vietnamese fusion food, and tries his hand at the national sport, Da Cau. Moving on to the Annamite Mountains west of Da Nang, Michael joins modern Vietnamese on holiday in a quirky facsimile of a medieval French village. Then, in the tailoring capital of Hoi An, Michael sees how Cham weavers make their distinctive brocade

programme.title: Lost Land of the Tiger
programme.desc: The expedition shifts to high altitude in the mountains of Bhutan as Gordon Buchanan captures footage of a snow leopard cub at more than 5,000m, while his heat-sensing camera picks up a big cat stalking through the camp in darkness. Steve Backshall treks to Tiger Mountain along the Tibetan border, where a remote tribe helps provide a close encounter with an elusive predator. As the expedition concludes, the team presents its findings to the Bhutanese prime minister

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The naturalist investigates whether animals and plants have developed an awareness of mathematics, including bamboos that flower exactly the same around the world. Plus, the story of a German horse named Hans that was declared a mathematical genius, but all was not as it seemed

programme.title: A Town Like Alice (1956)
programme.desc: Second World War PoW Jean Paget is sent on a gruelling forced march between Japanese labour camps, but finds comfort in her friendship with Australian captive Joe Harman. During the journey Joe is caught stealing, tied to a cross and left to die. After the conflict is over, Jean is startled to receive a letter postmarked in Joe's home town of Alice Springs. Jack Lee's drama, based on the novel by Nevil Shute, starring Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch, Takagi and Jean Anderson

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes the wildlife of the desert-like west and south of the island, an area where dry spells can last months and survival is a struggle. Verreaux's sifakas live among trees with potentially lethal spikes, while the baobab trees house mouse lemurs, primates that feed on the droppings of fluffy bugs. During the toughest time of year, gangs of ring-tailed lemurs try to raise their young, and as the rains arrive, the year-long life cycle of the Labord's chameleon begins

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz show in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's daughter has pups, bringing the Whiskers family to a total of 37. She must decide whether to keep the newborns or allow them to die so her own litter can survive. Meanwhile, wounded Shakespeare becomes separated from the group while searching for food

programme.title: Botham: The Legend of '81
programme.desc: A look back at Ian Botham's cricketing career, marking 30 years since the Ashes series that saw the former all-rounder at the peak of his powers. Botham started the series against Australia in 1981 as England captain, but resigned following a string of poor performances and media criticism, and what followed at Headingley, Edgbaston and Old Trafford led to the series becoming remembered as Botham's Ashes. This programme charts the player's highs and lows on and off the pitch, as well as focusing on his epic fundraising achievements and subsequent broadcasting career. With contributions from David Gower, Viv Richards, Mick Jagger and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling onto the capital city of Madrid, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th century's most shocking works of art, before making his way to the town of Huesca. Michael's final stop is on the border with France at Canfranc station, where he learns of the role the old terminus played in the evacuation of Jews during the Second World War

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: In 1977, Bella Abzug is appointed to head the revived Women's Commission, and has to organise the first ever National Women's Convention in Houston. Phyllis is determined to mobilise her own supporters to attend. Drama, starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: Exhausted from a long-distance drive from Illinois, Alice and Pamela arrive in Houston for the National Women's Conference to find their hotel overbooked and have to share a room. Finding herself surrounding by such a diverse group of women, Alice begins to understand the plight of others and why they are battling for the ERA. Drama, starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton goes on a caving expedition under the summit of Blaven

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: The story of the battle for Fallujah is told through the eyewitness accounts of journalists, soldiers and civilians. Reporter Dexter Filkins and photographer Ashley Gilbertson were embedded with US Marines, with Gilberston revealing how the decision to capture an image of an Iraqi sniper changed not only his life but the lives of the soldiers with him. Fallujah resident Nidhal Abed talks about the fateful moment when she left her home to take her son to the doctors just a few streets away

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades delves deeper into the stories of three previous fixes to show how immigration has shaped the UK, remembering the restoration of a pump organ from the Caribbean, a barber's pole with Cypriot links and an Iraqi samovar

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: a beachcombing scrap metal artist, a nautical resin worker, AND a fairy-tale sculptor try to create seaside-themed masterpieces for buyers from Whitstable

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's daughter has pups, bringing the Whiskers family to a total of 37. She must decide whether to keep the newborns or allow them to die so her own litter can survive. Meanwhile, wounded Shakespeare becomes separated from the group while searching for food

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Typhoo Tea factory on the Wirral, which he visited back in 2017, where production has been upped to produce 109 million bags a week. In reports from the programme's archives, Cherry Healey visits a tea processing plant in Kenya, and Ruth Goodman sheds light on how Second World War troops risked their lives to brew up

programme.title: Lost Land of the Jaguar
programme.desc: Scientists, climbers and wildlife film-makers explore the rainforests of Guyana, a former British colony in South America. Cameraman Gordon Buchanan finds giant otters almost as large as he is, canopy expert Justine Evans observes a family of red howler monkeys, and biologist Steve Backshall probes a habitat below a massive waterfall where frogs and freshwater crabs happily co-exist

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist investigates two shells that have proved to be winners in evolution - the bird's egg shell and the hard carapace of the tortoise. The ostrich egg is so strong it is possible for it to take a person's weight without breaking - so how does a chick manage break out of such a fortress? The evolution of the tortoise shell was for a long time a mystery, and this bony box offers a lot more than just protection

programme.title: Love Is a Many Splendored Thing (1955)
programme.desc: A married American news correspondent reporting on the Korean War from his base in Hong Kong falls in love with a widowed Eurasian doctor - but he is unable to get a divorce and the couple find themselves forced to keep their relationship a secret. Romantic drama based on the memoirs of Han Suyin, with William Holden, Jennifer Jones and Torin Thatcher

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley go head to head at a London car boot sale, where Caroline comes across some school chairs from the 1970s

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Leonora is a 16-year-old orangutan who has been living in a commune on a protected island run by the Borneo Orangutan Survival Foundation. This documentary follows Leonora and her three-year-old infant Lemar as they undertake an epic and life-changing journey by boat, road and finally helicopter, before being released into the rainforest

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Croome, a Palladian mansion in Worcestershire, where experts Adam Partridge and David Fletcher find a hand-operated fire pump

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The pups embark on a foraging mission, but find themselves in trouble when a giant eagle owl appears in search of a meal, and a thunderstorm threatens the family

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff lifeboat stations across the UK. A retired nursery teacher is in danger after trying to rescue her two dogs when they became cut off by the rising tide. In Wales, a volunteer is washed off a cliff after trying to rescue a concussed fisherman, and in Blackpool, three crew members' lives are in jeopardy

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The contestants are introduced to the world of theatrical make-up at London's smash hit The Lion King, where they try to precisely replicate the faces of the four main characters. To add to the pressure, the guest judge is Ashley Roller, the worldwide hair and make-up designer for the show. However, it's all to play for as the hopeful who impresses the most is invited to work alongside the make-up team for the evening's theatre performance

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of rest and recreation this week, with Stephen K Amos, Susan Calman, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: As Simon picks Rosie up from school, staff inform him they have noticed bruising on his daughter's legs and sides. Soon after he and Emily are told that social services will be paying a visit, prompting Simon to grow increasingly paranoid. In flashback, the couple are thrilled when Rosie takes her first steps but their joy gives way to concern when their daughter proves to be very accident prone. Starring David Tennant, Jessica Hynes and Miley Locke

programme.title: Mrs America
programme.desc: In the presidential election year 1979, Congress grants a three-year extension on the deadline for the signing of the Equal Rights Amendment. Phyllis presides over a political fundraiser where she is courted for her support, while Bella is fired by President Carter. Drama, starring Cate Blanchett, Rose Byrne, Margo Martindale, Uzo Aduba and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the first match in a three-game ODI series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. These sides last met in the 50-over format of the game in May 2019, when a one-off encounter at the Village in Malahide finished in a four-wicket win for England. Ben Foakes top-scored for the visitors with 61 not out, while Liam Plunkett took 4/35 from his seven overs in what was a straightforward victory for Eoin Morgan's men, who would famously go on to lift the World Cup on home soil two months later

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Rupert Murdoch's involvement in the 2016 US elections and the Brexit referendum, which saw the media mogul rising in prominence after a series of scandals and setbacks, from the death of his mother and the collapse of his second marriage to the loss of several important political allies. The programme examines the resolution of the question of Murdoch's successor and a final multi-billion dollar deal that surprises everyone

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades recalls three barn fixes - a butter churn, a spinning wheel and a set of ploughing trophies - to trace the changing face of rural life in the UK

programme.title: The Kemps: All True
programme.desc: Spoof documentary, starring Gary and Martin Kemp as themselves, following the brothers as they launch new careers in the wake of Spandau Ballet's 40th anniversary celebrations. While Martin tries to get a sci-fi movie franchise off the ground, Gary has a new brand of vegan meat substitute to promote

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The pups embark on a foraging mission, but find themselves in trouble when a giant eagle owl appears in search of a meal, and a thunderstorm threatens the family

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The seasons are marked on Wales' Pembrokeshire's coast by the arrival of the puffins, which court by tapping their multi-coloured bills. Farmers grow the earliest potatoes, while sheep are employed to maintain the clifftop pastures for the rare choughs that use their long red beaks to probe for grubs. As autumn arrives, the grey seals produce their white-coated pups just before the gales mark the might of winter. Using time-lapse techniques, this documentary brings fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the countryside in an exploration of how and why this region is the way it is. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Lost Land of the Jaguar
programme.desc: The team travels deeper into the Guyanan jungle and scorpions and poisonous centipedes enter the base camp. Gordon Buchanan discovers an animal is stealing supplies and Steve Backshall finds giant piranha and long-fanged sabretooth fish in the river. Meanwhile, George McGavin catches an enormous tarantula and the climbing team tries to scale a huge unclimbed mountain at the forest's edge

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: First-round coverage as the tournament gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, with Judd Trump beginning the defence of his title, and Stuart Bingham also in action

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: First-round coverage continues at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two more matches get under way in the showpiece tournament on the snooker calendar. This is also one of the highlights of the sporting year, which, like so many events in 2020, is taking place in vastly different circumstances to the norm. Several promising overseas players have been unable to enter the tournament, but former runner-up Ding Junhui is among those taking part, and he begins his campaign this afternoon against a qualifier

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The family is caught in a storm and forced to take shelter in a rival group's den - which causes a ruckus when the inhabitants return. Meanwhile, group leader Flower evicts one of the young females in an aggressive fashion

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session gets under way at the Crucible Theatre, where world number one and defending champion Judd Trump aims to book his place in the second round, and Mark Williams begins his match. Trump has enjoyed an outstanding season and is looking to break the 'Crucible Curse', and become the only first-time world champion to successfully defend his title at the first time of asking

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don refreshes a container of overgrown herbs, plants alpines in a pot and revisits the shrub and perennial cuttings he took a few weeks ago. Arit Anderson travels to Kent to meet a designer who is placing sustainability and the environment at the heart of the gardens she designs. Plus, a garden in Dorset that has been laid out specifically with scent in mind and an archive report by Frances Tophill who last year visited an allotment on the Isle of Man to meet a grower who uses plants to make cosmetics and there's a second chance to meet an enthusiast on the Isle of Wight who has filled his garden with daylilies

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: On the second leg of her journey, Miriam is intrigued by the Australian concept of 'mateship' and how Australia is the only country in the world to enshrine this in its culture. Her first stop is a roadhouse south of Alice Springs, where she meets one of the few women truckers in Australia. She also encounters Aboriginal elders, who set up the Children's Ground to pass Arrernte traditions, songs, dances and stories on to the younger generation. Arriving in Darwin, Miriam encounters a group of transgender Tiwi islanders, who invite her to a drag night

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Holly Johnson looks back at the year when the charts were dominated by Nik Kershaw, Madonna, Sade and his own band Frankie Goes to Hollywood, whose song Relax caused a storm of controversy. He reveals how Brat Pack stars Demi Moore, Rob Lowe and Emilio Estevez shot to fame, while Robin of Sherwood and The A-Team hit TV screens, the Care Bears melted young hearts and Trivial Pursuit sparked a new era in board games

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom tries talking Louise into helping him with a silly lie, but presenting a united front proves tricky. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban present analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on the opening day, where the first-round match featuring Judd Trump reached a conclusion, and the one involving Mark Williams got under way

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: An in-depth focus on the opening day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, as the season-ending championship got under way with the best-of-19-frames first-round matches

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the media mogul's influence on international politics and the power struggles within his own family. The first episode focuses on the build-up to the 1997 general election, where Rupert Murdoch had to decide if his business interests were best served by the continuation of John Major's struggling government, or a new Labour leadership under Tony Blair. Eventually, the full force of his media empire is placed behind the rising Labour leader, but some question how high a price was paid. Elsewhere, Murdoch marries a woman 37 years younger than he is, dividing his children and jeopardising his plans to choose a successor

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank travels to the desolate ruins of Amarna in search of the truth about Akhenaten, the most radical pharaoh ever to rule Egypt. He converted the nation to his own religion and built a sacred city in the desert where he lived with his wife Nefertiti until their dreams ended in tragedy

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross demonstrates how to create the illusion of a mountain on a bright day mirrored in still waters

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Riviera's artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote d'Azur's beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent explores the sights, sounds and schedules of the Great British Summer, an epic journey that covers everything that informs viewers attitudes to summertime. She looks back at a range of influential television, from the travel shows of the sixties and seventies that first brought the world's finest resorts into our living rooms, to Del and Rodney Trotter fooling about abroad

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Former jockey Fred Quilley becomes convinced he's being hunted by a gang of crooks who once bribed him to fix a race. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, Felix Bowness and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A celebration of the British seaside holiday experience, from its Victorian origins and heyday in the 1950s, through its slow decline and attempts at modern-day reinvention

programme.title: Andrew Marr's Great Scots: The Writers Who Shaped a Nation
programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist looks into the life of 20th-century poet Hugh MacDiarmid, a staunch nationalist who reinvented Scots as a language for serious writing and tried to create an independent Scottish communist utopia. He was for many years under surveillance by MI5, was involved in plots to capture Edinburgh Castle and steal the Stone of Destiny, but also wrote the most powerful poetry in Scots since the days of Robert Burns, and started a Scottish renaissance that goes on to this day

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of 'Crazy' Fakhir Berwari, a Kurdish colonel in the Iraq Army and renowned bomb disposal expert, who single-handedly disarmed thousands of landmines across the country with just a pocketknife and a pair of wire clippers. Between the fall of Saddam Hussein in 2003 and the chaos and destruction wreaked by Isis 10 years later, Fakhir's unwavering bravery saved thousands of lives throughout his nation. This film tells Fakhir's story through the raw and visceral amateur footage captured by his soldiers on camcorder

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Emily takes Rosie on a disastrous trip to the swimming pool, leaving her trying to work out what Rosie really wants, while Simon's behaviour causes raised eyebrows from some of the other parents when he goes to take pictures of all the local playgrounds and paddling pools. In the earlier timeline, Rosie goes to her first genetics appointment where, in contrast to Emily's scientific detachment, Simon becomes angry that she is treated like an interesting puzzle to be solved

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: Grandad John and Nana Annie want to take Ben away over the summer, but Simon and Emily want to know why they will not take Rosie as well. In the earlier timeline, Simon and Emily take the kids to Grandad John's for Christmas, but their relationship is at breaking point. As Emily sinks to her lowest ebb, it's Grandad John who finally gives her the support she needs

programme.title: There She Goes
programme.desc: A birthday meal out at a restaurant descends into chaos, but luckily Grandma has bought Rosie presents to distract her. Starring David Tennant and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American painter Bob Ross demonstrates how to create the illusion of a mountain on a bright day mirrored in still waters

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The presenter examines the pyramids of Saqqara and Giza, and the process of mummification the Egyptians used to keep their bodies intact long after death. He explains how their belief in the afterlife led to the need to keep themselves and their possessions whole in order to pass into the next world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a seascape with a dramatic sky that marks an approaching storm, setting the mood with a canvas painted half black and half white

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The heroine plans a ball with Frank, and wonders whether she has feelings for him. Despite having a wonderful time at the event, she still feels trapped, and with the matchmaking effort officially abandoned, becomes increasingly bored. However, her spirits are raised by Mr Knightley's proposal for a group outing to Box Hill, where he tells her he thinks Frank and Jane are in love. Drama, starring Romola Garai and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Becoming Jane (2007)
programme.desc: A young Jane Austen's parents are intent on finding her a wealthy husband, but she remains determined to marry for love. Before long, a dashing but penniless lawyer catches the future novelist's eye, but any suggestion of romance meets with distinct disapproval from their families. Romantic period drama, with Anne Hathaway, James McAvoy, Julie Walters, Maggie Smith and Ian Richardson

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke reveals how the Romans' obsession with sex and violence was reflected in their art, and dons a wetsuit to visit the remains of emperor Claudius's pleasure palace. He also examines the bombastic pieces the Empire has become renowned for, including monumental arches and columns whose imagery relays the story of glorious military victories. Plus, the story of the emperor Hadrian, who redesigned the Pantheon in Rome

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines the age of the Romantics, and the flamboyant George IV who created so much of the visual look of the modern monarchy. He also looks back the lives of at Queen Victoria and Prince Albert, for whom collecting was an integral part of their happy marriage

programme.title: Bought with Love: The Secret History of British Art Collections
programme.desc: Helen Rosslyn concludes her exploration of impressive country houses, focusing on how collecting art reached its maturity in the 19th century when unprecedented wealth from Britain's economy encouraged enlightened, philanthropic industrialists to spend their fortunes on it. She focuses on the influence of pharmaceutical magnate Thomas Holloway, the Rothschild banking dynasty, and welsh sisters Gwendoline and Margaret Davies

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a seascape with a dramatic sky that marks an approaching storm, setting the mood with a canvas painted half black and half white

programme.title: Egyptian Journeys with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The presenter examines the pyramids of Saqqara and Giza, and the process of mummification the Egyptians used to keep their bodies intact long after death. He explains how their belief in the afterlife led to the need to keep themselves and their possessions whole in order to pass into the next world

programme.title: The Treasures of Ancient Rome
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke reveals how the Romans' obsession with sex and violence was reflected in their art, and dons a wetsuit to visit the remains of emperor Claudius's pleasure palace. He also examines the bombastic pieces the Empire has become renowned for, including monumental arches and columns whose imagery relays the story of glorious military victories. Plus, the story of the emperor Hadrian, who redesigned the Pantheon in Rome

programme.title: Gershwin's Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heyward's song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mike Read and Paul Gambaccini present the edition first broadcast on 28 December, featuring performances by Kim Wilde, Aswad and Band Aid II. Plus, Bros, Duran Duran, the Pet Shop Boys, Michael Jackson, Phil Collins, Kylie Minogue and many others feature in a reflective look at hits from the entire decade

programme.title: Gospel According to Mica - The Story of Gospel Music in Six Songs
programme.desc: Soul singer Mica Paris examines the origins of the gospel songs she sang as a child in Lewisham, south London. She investigates why some of her contemporaries have returned to their gospel roots and reconsiders her earlier decision to embrace secular music. Mica meets the choir at Fisk University in Tennessee, who once sang for Queen Victoria, and finds out how the slavery freedom fighters used gospel to communicate. She recalls Sam Cooke and Thomas A Dorsey, who both encountered tragedy after leaving the Church, and hears of current artists, such as Stormzy, who happily embrace both church and secular music

programme.title: The Last Pirates: Britain's Rebel DJs
programme.desc: Rodney P's career as a rapper would not have been possible without the lifeblood of pirate radio airplay in the 1980s. Here, he presents an alternative history of Britain during a time of entrepreneurialism and social upheaval, with archive and music that celebrates a very different side of Thatcher's Britain. His story details how land-based pirate radio broadcasters created a platform for black music, changing the soundtrack of Britain for ever. Featuring contributions by DJs, station owners and Department of Trade and Industry enforcers

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of Back to Black, the second and final studio album by singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, which was released in October 2006 to wide critical and commercial acclaim. Featuring singles Rehab, You Know I'm No Good, Back to Black, Tears Dry on Their Own and Love Is a Losing Game, the record has sold approximately four million copies

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: A Prom from July 2015 at the Royal Albert Hall, when Radio 1 DJ Pete Tong employed the Heritage Orchestra to play a selection of Ibiza classics. Jules Buckley conducts through dance masterpieces by Fatboy Slim, Eric Prydz, Shapeshifters, Robert Miles, ATB, Moby, Frankie Knuckles, Orbital, Inner City, Daft Punk, Faithless, Stardust and others, with the help of singers Ella Eyre and John Newman

programme.title: I Can Go for That: The Smooth World of Yacht Rock
programme.desc: Katie Puckrik charts the progress of the genre through the 1980s, when it became the soundtrack to America in the Reagan era, and when artists like Toto, Hall & Oates and George Benson created a technicolour second wave of a super smooth sound. In the second of two programmes, yacht session supremos Jay Graydon and Steve Porcaro recall working with Benson. Porcaro joined other LA session players to form Toto and he and Steve Lukather reveal how they brought a little yacht magic to the biggest-selling album in history - Michael Jackson's Thriller. Other contributors include Robbie Dupree and John Oates

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: Jimmy Iovine talks about hitting a wall and how he considered his own career shift after a particularly difficult collaboration with U2, whose tireless drive in the studio rivaled his own. By 1989, Jimmy had parlayed his production expertise into a new career as co-founder of Interscope Records, committing the label to artists such as Trent Reznor's Nine Inch Nails, Marilyn Manson, Gwen Stefani and No Doubt. Dr Dre discusses a series of calamities he experienced including personal losses on the streets and run-ins with police authorities, as well as a bitter contract dispute and a clash over management that strained Dre's relationship with Eazy-E. He also recalls landing at a crossroads and looking to make a fresh start

programme.desc: Animated antics with the adventurous aardvark and his friends

programme.desc: When Gluko and Lennon accidentally throw their friend Sheena into the scariest place ever, Gluko encourages Lennon to face his fears in order to save her.

programme.desc: Things get hot in the kitchen as the restaurant fills with hungry diners, including Strictly star AJ Pritchard. And two contestants from each crew go through to the semi-finals

programme.desc: Xuli finds a shipwreck on a coral reef, apparently leaking oil. However, as it is raised an octopus swims out squirting ink

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Tommy Brock steal the rabbits' go- kart, with Cotton-tail on board, Peter needs to race faster than ever to rescue his sister!

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Bob are making a film about Big Pop

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts use the Octopod to transport a pair of invasive lionfish back to their native habitat

programme.title: Oti's Boogie Beebies
programme.desc: In this episode, Oti teaches us how to happy bounce!

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin is in a singing mood, so Robert uses a new invention to help his friend pick his favourite song

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: The fidgety lion waits for the beautiful Bell-Flowers to open

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Harriet shows the class how to bake cookies and Timmy decides he will make one bigger than all the others put together and decorate it to look like a football

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A talking ride on car seems like a fun toy at Amma's creche, but Bing and Sula must use their imagination when it crashes

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The twirlywoos discover playing with a radio can have noisy consequences, and the Stop-Go Car comes to visit

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The friends look at their reflections in a puddle, but Dipsy is sad when his hat falls in and gets wet

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with potatoes and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels enjoy a day at the seaside when they are suddenly dwarfed by a giant, regal sailing ship that has ground onto the beach. The squirrels go to investigate, encountering penguins who explain they were on a very important mission when they got sick. They need to head to a nearby island but they are too poorly to sail the ship

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: The monsters have fun building balloon-powered boats in class. As they splash lots of water about, Icklewoo suddenly needs the toilet, but is too scared to go and has a little accident. Luckily, Mrs Twirlyhorn steps in and shows her the importance of asking a grown up for help

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: It is Sid's turn for a fun-filled adventure as he plays a show jumper and tries to win a competition by beating the favourite, Mary Ann Fetlock

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a walk to play with pinwheels in the wind.

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Mouse finds the noisy colours of the supermarket overwhelming. Pablo and his friends try to help, putting her in the trolley and letting her look at the lovely quiet list

programme.title: Tee and Mo Song Time
programme.desc: Tee and Mo can't go out of the house at the moment, so they find things to keep them busy

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: A fable about why baboons have distinctive bottoms. Based on a collection of African folk tales

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Charlotte makes an Irish boxty for her friends, by using mashed and grated potatoes to make her boxty and topping it with a cooked egg

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells take Waffle to Mrs Hobbs' house, hoping to show her he's well behaved. With the voice of Rufus Hound

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb searches for wild animals in the nursery and is disappointed not to find any

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The adventurous duo plant some seeds after getting them for free in a newspaper

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee insists on wearing his favourite Action Ape outfit all afternoon, no matter what games he is playing. However, when the outfit gets dirty, Tee is heartbroken

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The discovery of some invisible ink leads to all manner of tricks being played on the Alphablocks

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: A look at bikes doing lots of different things - hopping, shopping, dashing and flashing. Dee encourages a friend to say 'bike', and a whirlwind word adventure lies in store

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One, Two, Three and Four go on a castle-exploring adventure, with a little help from the friends they make along the way

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: It is a windy day and Bing wants to fly his kite with Flop, but insists on doing it without help. When the toy gets caught in a park bench, the rabbit needs an extra pair of hands

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos accidentally misplace cupcakes, and the children must go on a hunt to find them

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend become animal keepers at an animal park. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble looks after an animal too - his friend, Spotty the horse

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The quartet take it in turns to go up and down on the Dup Dup. Sometimes they go fast and sometimes they go slow - even The Noo-noo has a turn

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble spots a lonely star in the night sky, but even Groan Up cannot reach it. The Olobobs invent Skyder who carries them up to space, and the little star is lonely no more

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The youngster is woken by loud banging and scraping, and decides all that racket could only be made by a fearsome 'noise dragon'

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends learn about love by looking at the things and people around him, and how they make him feel

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Twins Daisy and Lily take a trip to the beach with their cousins. Dogs Foxy and Saffy join in too, and love chasing after balls on the sand. After all the seaside fun, the whole family heads to the beach cafe to enjoy an ice-cream. Back at home, the sisters take care of a special visitor - Grandma Owl the school cuddly toy. Together they embark on an adventure around the house and take lots of photos

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies rally driver Catie Running shows off three amazing machines that all have sails

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line prove to be absolutely hopeless at tying knots, so Captain Captain gives them her own 'Little Book of Nautical Knots' to practice their skills

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly is digging in the garden when his spade suddenly hits a key. Duggee has his key badge, so they can find out through trial and error what it opens. The squirrels go on an adventure to work out what the key opens and what could be inside

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When the rabbits are forced out of their secret tree-house, they learn that finding a new hideout isn't the hard part - it's keeping it secret

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Princess Purl and Princess Bitz have decided to create the best party ever for Sir Robo Bob and Sir Bevel

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The team heads to the ancient Peruvian city of Machu Picchu, to watch Kyan compete in the Inca Trail Marathon

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go strawberry picking and make jam to preserve the berries for longer.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran fix up Gran Gran's old bike and cycle through the park together.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The friends go for a trip to the zoo after Duck says he wants to be a penguin

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After Colly Wobble loses her shows, Moon Baby shows her a magical shoe tree in Storyland

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small notices that the Iron Chicken has become trapped by junk metal that has got caught in orbit around her nest, but Major comes up with a clever solution

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy teaches Igglepiggle how to kick a ball around the garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas reads the story of Betty, Cosmo and their adventures.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
