programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A blood donor session sees some awkward encounters, while Ash annoys a customer. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen reach the final leg of their round-Wales adventure by canal, one which has a personal connection for them. The narrowboat novices grew up at opposite ends of the same street in the village of Gwaun-cae-Gurwen, near Neath. As children, they would play in the areas by the derelict canals, but knew very little of them and their history. The Swansea canal and the Neath and Tennant canals reveal a story of industry that once dominated the area, getting coal and iron to the docks. Now virtually impassable, there are plans afoot to give them a new lease of life

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks at mid-terrace houses in Stoke-on-Trent and Wolverhampton and Martin Roberts explores a large listed building in Rochester

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A group of hikers whose Dartmoor walk turned serious when one of them fell and landed on rocks below. Plus, footage of a couple and their baby as they fled from a hotel fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Tim Weeks and John Cameron help two teams find items to sell for a profit at auction at the Kent County Showground. Charlie Ross pays a visit to Leeds Castle

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kat's contact with Patch causes alarm. Kam starts his new school. Australian drama, starring Siria Kickett and Yazeed Daher

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a house hunting mission in the Yorkshire Dales with a pair of brothers, one of whom wants his £500,000 budget to furnish him with a country home in rural isolation. While in the area, Nicki joins the Dales Park Rangers and helps them on their everyday rounds as well, as learning about the growing popularity of geocaching with walkers

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Morecambe Bay, where they get to meet to meet the buyer's cherished horse, Dragon, before pitching potential artworks inspired by the animal

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Popular quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace are joined by Italian chef Aldo Zilli, who challenges the six remaining hopefuls to recreate one of his favourite recipes. After battling with unfamiliar ingredients and techniques, the next task sees them serving up a dish inspired by someone they love or admire - knowing that only three of them will be awarded places in the final

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2020 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Over the past few months, more than 20,000 care home residents and staff have died of Covid-19 across the country. Panorama follows the struggle to protect residents, filming in two different homes over several months. What it reveals is the dedication of staff, the frustration of managers and heartache as more lives are lost. Alison Holt asks if care homes were abandoned to fight the virus alone

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Kate Prince is a remarkable British dance pioneer whose renowned ZooNation company has transformed the profile of hip-hop and street dance across the country. Alan Yentob meets the choreographer as she embarks on her latest project, Message in a Bottle, a West End production set to the songs of Sting. The programme follows the highs and lows of birthing a complex new dance show from day one in the studio to opening night, while also exploring the wider impact of Prince's work and politics on a younger generation of upcoming dancers

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out on a quest to discover the meaning of art. It's a long strange trip into a world of giant inflatables, edible sculptures and flying doughnuts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian steps out of the spotlight to learn another trade, trying his hand at the distinctly glamourless profession of farming - and his introduction to the job sees him mucking out the cows

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three artists travel to Morecambe Bay, where they get to meet to meet the buyer's cherished horse, Dragon, before pitching potential artworks inspired by the animal

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson travel around Yorkshire, finding such rarities as a vintage set of weights and a Victorian skirt lifter, before an auction in Rotherham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Popular quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: In the second semi-final, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace are joined by Italian chef Aldo Zilli, who challenges the six remaining hopefuls to recreate one of his favourite recipes. After battling with unfamiliar ingredients and techniques, the next task sees them serving up a dish inspired by someone they love or admire - knowing that only three of them will be awarded places in the final

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The three remaining celebrities take part in the 2020 final, in which they must cook and present a faultless three-course meal for judgement by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, in what is their last chance to prove they have what it takes to be crowned champion

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Over the past few months, more than 20,000 care home residents and staff have died of Covid-19 across the country. Panorama follows the struggle to protect residents, filming in two different homes over several months. What it reveals is the dedication of staff, the frustration of managers and heartache as more lives are lost. Alison Holt asks if care homes were abandoned to fight the virus alone

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Kate Prince is a remarkable British dance pioneer whose renowned ZooNation company has transformed the profile of hip-hop and street dance across the country. Alan Yentob meets the choreographer as she embarks on her latest project, Message in a Bottle, a West End production set to the songs of Sting. The programme follows the highs and lows of birthing a complex new dance show from day one in the studio to opening night, while also exploring the wider impact of Prince's work and politics on a younger generation of upcoming dancers

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.desc: The freak wave that capsized the boat of two young men attempting to set a world record rowing the Atlantic. Plus, the family day out in the snow that almost ended in tragedy when a man suffered an accident while sledging

programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A mother and daughter suddenly find their tumble drier has set alight, and it all goes wrong for a top British surfer as he rides a wave

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall guide two teams scouring south-west London for bargains to take to auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to Strawberry Hill House in Twickenham, one of the finest examples of Gothic revival architecture in the UK

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple of retired teachers search for a property in North Yorkshire with a budget of £450,000, and she also meets with a young dairy farmer

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three artists in Birmingham to create a piece for a mystery buyer to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion travel around Yorkshire to find items to sell at auction. Charles finds a exciting artefact in a toilet, and Christina falls for a silk gown

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Michael Ball

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. Also, cameras follow Border Force officers intercepting potentially lethal medicines and members of the National Crime Agency as they raid the homes of currency counterfeiters. Presented by Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in the first semi-final, travelling to the National Breeding Centre at Leamington Spa's Guide Dogs For The Blind to face their toughest challenge so far. Cooking in tents and faced with unfamiliar equipment, the celebrities are split into two teams and challenged to cook up a feast to celebrate Guide Dogs For The Blind's 90th anniversary for 120 staff, volunteers and those whose lives have been changed by the organisation. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the competitors are tasked with recreating their favourite takeaway dishes in the Invention Test

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The men of East Belfast Yacht Club transform old hulks into ocean-going vessels, but before they can get out to the open water they have to navigate them under a bridge

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: A group of 10 young people try to kickstart their careers over one season on the party island of Ibiza, spending six weeks living together in workers apartments. From a naked butler and a VIP host, to teetotal vegans and a party girl, the youngsters move in determined work hard and follow their dreams in the face of non-stop temptation

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look back at the finalists' route to Saturday's showpiece match, which will take place behind closed doors at Wembley Stadium due to the ongoing Coronavirus pandemic. This season's staging of the famous competition began way back in August 2019, before the Premier League and Championship clubs joined the fray as usual in January at the third-round stage. Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal and holders Manchester City made it to the semi-finals, and all four teams will have been eager to lift the prestigious trophy, especially given Liverpool's dominance of the Premier League

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Manchester City winger Shaun Wright-Phillips, 1992 Olympic 400m hurdles champion Sally Gunnell, seven-time world snooker champion Stephen Hendry, and 2016 Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Jazz Carlin join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Belfast Harbour: Cruises, Cranes & Cargo
programme.desc: Documentary series following life at Belfast Harbour, with access to a hidden world of crane drivers, boat pilots, tour guides and mega ships. Elsewhere, Robert and Karen, two of the shipyard's workers, tell their stories

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: Sarah enacts a plan on Nicola's advice, Andrea is in a state of bliss, and Jamie's mask slips

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ed is gutted that Aggie has to isolate at a hotel on their 30th wedding anniversary, and Sarah demands to know if Gary killed Rick, before he is hit by a car. Elaine calls Alya and confirms that she's prepared to give evidence against Geoff

programme.title: Paul O'Grady for the Love of Dogs: What Happened Next
programme.desc: The comedian catches up with some of the most memorable dogs he has met from the eight years he has spent at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home. This time, there is a heart-warming update with Chester, the most injured dog Paul's ever helped, and Louis the Labrador, who now works at a medical detection school. Later, Paul discovers how Sydney, Battersea's most famous rescue and firm favourite of actor Tom Hardy, has coped in the outside world

programme.title: Anne: The Princess Royal at 70
programme.desc: A special documentary marking Princess Anne's 70th birthday, as she talks openly about her life and work, and the reality of growing up as a member of the Royal family. Throughout the programme, Princess Anne talks frankly about a family in which women tended to be seen as 'honorary men', about the challenges for younger royalty, about a bloody kidnap attempt, and about refusing titles for her children, Peter and Zara

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The TV presenter looks back on her life and career, opens up about the death of her son William in a car accident when he was just 19, and reflects on how she has become one of the nation's most recognisable faces in her seventies. She also talks about cooking cow-udder pie for her first TV appearance more than 40 years ago, and reveals how she used to discipline her children

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef rides his motorbike through the coastal city of Santa Cruz, California. He shops at local farmers' markets before cooking his recipes on a family run artichoke farm. James also tries his hand at surfing, but gets upstaged by a dog

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A motorbike gets stuck up a tree, a woman receives an electric shock from a fly zapper and a pensioner ends up on TV - after the TV ends up on top of them

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh is in Newton Abbot, South Devon, with the Glen family. Despite following a meat-free diet, no one eats the same thing, making mealtimes difficult - on top of which they are spending hundreds of pounds a week. In addition, chef Dale Pinnock questions mum Jena's daily calorie intake, while personal trainer Tom Pitfield tackles dad Aaron's attitude to exercise. Will the Glens make the most of the eight-week challenge?

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues his journey around the Wales Coast Path as he travels around the wild Llyn Peninsula. He takes the helm of a 1930s sailing ship to navigate the notorious Menai Strait. This narrow stretch of water, which separates Anglesey from the rest of north Wales, has a reputation for being difficult to navigate - but luckily Sean is joined by ex-pilot boat skipper Emrys Williams. Moving on, Sean also hits the waves with adaptive-surfer Llywelyn Williams, who lost his leg in a car accident, and meets a woman who walked the entire path with her donkey

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three factories in Yorkshire. It reveals how Aunt Bessie's produces millions of Yorkshire puddings a year, how Yorkshire Tea turn out teabags by the truckload and how Birds Eye workers face a race against time to get their peas from field to freezer. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary allowing a peek at the workings the Ritz, one of the most famous luxury hotels in the world. Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill puts his own spin on viewers' clips. Tonight, a bungled balcony theft in the Home Counties, why not to use light bulbs as shuttlecocks, and the world's most inappropriate birthday present

programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play Dizzy Dash, Son of a One Eyed Monster, You Bet Your Wife, Blindfolded Musical Chairs and Know or Go

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Josh, Scott and Tim go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Sarah from Lancashire

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring home-made bombs, an emu stealing a smartphone, and tips on how not to trim a tree. Plus, Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield lookalikes in an amusing situation

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the game show, asking contestants Adrian Edmondson, Jason Manford and Jenny Powell to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Guest host Justin Hartley welcomes Ciara, who discusses America's Most Musical Family. Hannah Brown talks about Dancing with the Stars and Chris Janson performs Good Vibes

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Ellen DeGeneres brings her love for fun to the stage with super-sized versions of some of the most popular games from her daytime show

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by 2019 Love Islanders Anton and Belle, Ovie and India and Chris and Harley as he hosts a special edition of the game show. The three couples go wild in the aisles as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize for charity

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Suitors George, Justin and Lewis compete for the affections of Londoner Jen. Which of the three single shoppers' fashion choices will win them a date?

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a man falling off a bridge into a canal, a lion jumping inside a car on safari and a student finding an unwelcome surprise in her toilet. Plus, Peter Stringfellow uses a paintball gun to fire Viagra, Harry reveals the real reason Zayn left One Direction and Caitlin Moran falls off a catamaran. Narrated by Harry Hill

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another round of viewer-submitted comedy clips, as captured via smartphone, drone and CCTV. This time, a hoverboard destroys an office, a man falls through a casino ceiling, and an ostrich takes a shine to a young child's mobile phone

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another selection of viewer-submitted mishaps, captured in video form via smartphones, drones and other home-recording equipment. This time, a drone is destroyed by an errant baseball, a seal refuses to join in on a selfie, and a falling tree traps what seems to be a familiar face in the toilet

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina blackmails Garrett after Amy hires Dina's nemesis Coleen. Meanwhile, Jonah is forced to become friends with Marcus as he tries to unionise the warehouse staff

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The closure of the local shopping centre brings an influx of new customers - but it turns out they have not come to buy. Cheyenne learns a difficult truth

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. Another power cut gives Peter the opportunity to regale the family with a second Star Wars-themed story, this time based on Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back. Chris, Meg, Lois, Brian and Stewie are recast in their original roles, while Mort is introduced as fan-favourite Lando Calrissian and Chris's boss Carl fills in for Yoda. With the voices of Seth MacFarlane, Seth Green and Mila Kunis

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part two of two. Another power cut gives Peter the opportunity to regale the family with a second Star Wars-themed story, this time based on Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back. Chris, Meg, Lois, Brian and Stewie are recast in their original roles, while Mort is introduced as fan-favourite Lando Calrissian and Chris's boss Carl fills in for Yoda. With the voices of Seth MacFarlane, Seth Green and Mila Kunis

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian learns Rush Limbaugh has arranged a book signing at the Quahog Mall, and decides to attend the event to give the outspoken radio talk-show host a piece of his mind. However, when Rush unexpectedly comes to his rescue, the liberal-thinking dog has a political change of heart

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Stewie are accidentally locked inside a bank vault for a weekend in the 150th episode of the popular adult animation, and their situation goes from bad to worse when Brian reveals his plans for the future

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter has a palm reading and discovers he led a past life as a gentleman in 17th-century England, where the fearsome Stewart III reigned. He learns how he tried to win the heart of Lady Redbush by following her to the newly founded American colony of Quahog. With the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve is selected to sing the national anthem at a Veterans Day celebration, but Stan is convinced he is not ready to undertake such a prestigious task, and decides he will only be capable of performing with feeling once he has experienced war first-hand

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: With a two-on-two basketball tournament approaching, Roger's alter-ego, local basketball legend 'Billy Jesusworth' breaks his ankle and is unable to compete at his usual level. Stan reluctantly invites him to play with his league of 'old heads', but his act of kindness backfires when Roger steals away Stan's tournament partner

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna insists the family helps at the homeless shelter at Christmas, but Cleveland is surprised to find the food there is better than what he eats at home. Meanwhile, Roberta is hired as an elf at a department store, but her chauvinistic employer requires her and other female employees to wear skimpy outfits

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland feels hurt when his dad, Freight Train, seems to ignore him in favour of spending time with Junior. When he learns the reason why, he decides to search for a new father figure - leading to a tense showdown at a golf tournament. Meanwhile, Rallo purchases a new sports car

programme.desc: Cheyenne and Mateo wonder if any of the employees are capable of murder, while Amy spruces up the shop's Halloween efforts, setting up a haunted house that leaves Glenn terrified

programme.title: Timewasters

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility

programme.desc: Action-packed game show, with commentary from Irish comedy duo the Rubberbandits. Ten contestants are given 50 lives to take on a series of tricky challenges. programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities and home-video howlers, featuring golfing gaffes, canine calamities, animal antics, holiday howlers and amusing CCTV footage. Presented by Lisa Riley

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another selection of comical mishaps filmed by viewers. This week, a mechanic uses an airbag as a launch-pad. A moped selfie causes a nasty accident that results in a string of profanities, and a peck from a tropical bird make's Grandad's eyes water. Plus, a video detailing the dangers of wearing a monkey as a wig

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Ellen DeGeneres brings her love for fun to the stage with supersized versions of some of the most popular games from her daytime show.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Opera singer and model Paige from Hertfordshire returns to the show to be dressed by three male suitors. Whose outfit will she choose to wear on the date?

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates more comical mishaps filmed by viewers. This week, a building crushes a luxury sports car, and a tree manages to topple a motorcyclist. Plus, danger is afoot as a shark tries to bite a woman at the beach

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: A special edition of the game show in which host Stephen Mulhern invites comedy pairings to take place. Inside No 9 stars Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, Danny John-Jules and Robert Llewellyn of Red Dwarf fame and Nina Wadia and Lynda Baron, who co-star in Still Open All Hours, are the famous duos taking part. As usual they try to guess the popular phrases in the hope of scoring enough points to win a cash prize for their charity of choice - and making it through to the Super Catchphrase to play for £50,000

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Guest host Howie Mandel is joined by Ewan McGregor, who talks about starring in Doctor Sleep, a follow-up to classic horror The Shining

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen DeGeneres' studio audience play her outrageously fun games - including Dizzy Dash, Son of a One-Eyed Monster, Blindfolded Musical Chairs and Runaway Bride

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Healthcare co-ordinator Brad from Pontefract in West Yorkshire puts his faith in the fashion choices of Ashley, James and Mike

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage from the You've Been Framed! archives featuring children, from messy eaters to troublesome teens and sibling rivalries

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates as the programme features the pick of its archive of viewer-supplied animal clips, including a sunbathing panda, a cat masseuse and a flatulent horse

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy loses face in front of the district manager, so Jonah and Glenn help her hatch a plan to look like a tough boss. Dina and Mateo run a shoplifter rehabilitation programme

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy and Jonah help Mateo's toy drive in an attempt to make him look good ahead of his immigration hearing, and Jerry and Sandra ask Glenn to officiate their wedding

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire takes parental responsibility for a baby who looks like him, but realises he may not be suitable for fatherhood, and Stewie clones himself so that he can have a personal assistant

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: The family takes Hayley on a trip to look at colleges and leaves Steve home alone. He and his friends find what they think is a video game in Stan's study - but it turns out to be a CIA drone

programme.desc: Stan resurrects President Garfield to teach Hayley about history, and Steve starts writing for his school's newspaper. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna and Cleveland Jr compete in a dance contest - but just as they are set to take to the floor, the youngster makes an embarrassing admission that threatens their chances. Meanwhile, Roberta receives a harsh lesson about being a grown-up as she takes care of Rallo for a week

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland decides to form a ventriloquism act, but does not have a dummy and hits on the bright idea of using a thinly disguised Rallo - whose quick-witted comments earn them rave reviews. Unfortunately, their success soon leads to friction

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Joyce tries to persuade Vera to take on Judy as a barmaid. Don lays down the law when Kevin questions his authority. Liz, Deirdre, Audrey, Alma and Maureen have a girls' night in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is furious when Steve patronises her. The men go to see Betty's Hot Shot race at Thirsk. Maureen is stunned to see a for sale sign up outside Reg's flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Dave's funeral arrives. Zak attempts to win back Lisa's favour. Kathy attacks Kim when she turns up at the funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Kim she is not wanted at the funeral. Marlon and Butch vie for Sophie's attention. Roy tries to smash up Kim's car and blames her for killing his brother

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Meanwhile, a reluctant Bernie is taken on a mini break to a country hotel by Vernon - but he grows increasingly dismayed over the plans for a new garage. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby asks Philip for a native charm to help him seduce the delectable Ruth - but his dreams of a romantic liaison soon go up in smoke. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens tell Rachel about their financial problems. Vic invites Viv to dinner to discuss their children. Steve finally tells Rachel how he really feels about her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic appears very tolerant of the idea of Donna moving in with Viv and Terry. Chris offers to buy Kim out of the stables and the golf course. Jack is paid a visit by Andy's teacher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve thinks the police are using Vicky to get to him. Jim appears in court. Vicky turns to Alec for help, telling him everything. Maureen claims Reg's flat as her marital home.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve visits Malcolm Fox to find out what is going on. Maureen has the locks changed on Reg's flat. Ashley and Kelly plan to spend the night together when Ken goes away on a course

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor is found dead on Halloween with a stake through her heart and Hobson reveals she shared a house with the victim in her university days. A medium visits Lewis claiming to have foreseen the tragedy, and ghostly messages spelled out in fridge magnet letters start appearing in a house occupied by four students. As the detective struggles with the possibility his friend is somehow implicated in the crime, a tale of jealousy and revenge begins to unfold. Rupert Graves (Sherlock) and Niamh Cusack (Heartbeat) guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star

Starring Duncan Bell, Sarah Tansey and Aislin McGuckin

programme.desc: Mandy tells Zak that Lisa should come and live with the Dingles. Kathy is shocked when Kim comes to visit her and wants the two of them to be friends

programme.desc: Vicky tells Nick she is willing to take the blame to stop Steve going to prison. Alec plans to view Reg's flat, but Maureen tells him it's no longer for sale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken goes to Keele leaving Kelly looking after Daniel. Don spends the day at the garage, observing how it works

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mr Carson and Mrs Hughes' wedding is fast approaching, but a misunderstanding threatens to derail the occasion, and when the big day arrives it also brings a few unexpected guests. Meanwhile, Edith reconnects with an old acquaintance, who comes to her aid in her hour of need, and over at the Dower House, Spratt has something to hide, meaning Denker is hot on his trail. Daisy jumps to conclusions about one of the countess's plots, and tensions mount over the hospital takeover, prompting Isobel to offend a friend. Period drama, starring Jim Carter, Phyllis Logan, Elizabeth McGovern and Laura Carmichael

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Two children go on trial for killing a toddler, each blaming the other, and the case hinges on whether forensic evidence can prove who is guilty of strangling the boy to death. CPS director George Castle defies his superiors and takes instruction from the victim's mother, who has a surprising point of view on the crime. Drama, guest starring Deborah Findlay (Cranford), with Bill Paterson

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers Nick has been brutally assaulted - and she is top of the list of suspects. But even worse, she has no recollection of the previous night after a drunken hen party, and struggles to convince herself she did not do it. Janet thinks she has made her peace with Andy, until he gives her some misinformation that results in Gill missing an appointment with the coroner - so the DCI, tired of their games, announces one of them will have to go. A man's accidental death turns into a murder investigation when his daughters reveal their mother often talked of killing him. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert's got a new job as a GP in Yorkshire, but how will he tell Ida that they're moving away?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is a tense day in the Glover household when the inquest into Dave's death begins. Chris urges Frank to prove that James is his child by getting a paternity test

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth forgets Betty's birthday leading him to pretend he has a surprise planned. Jan apologises for hitting Kathy. Zak tidies the Dingle household after inviting Lisa for dinner

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily sees Ashley leaving Ken's first thing in the morning. Deirdre discovers Alec is short staffed and offers her services. Steve tells Fiona he was wrong to go off with Vicky

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Vicky go before the Magistrates charged with perverting the course of justice. Ken tells Kelly she must be careful about what goes on in his house

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tom and Sybbie return to Downton, but the peace is disrupted by an unexpected visit from an old friend. Baxter is faced with a dilemma after an encounter with Sergeant Willis, while Thomas struggles to fill Carson's shoes. Anna goes to London with Mary to deal with a crisis, while her secrets seem likely to be exposed. Daisy is not happy with Cora, and Mary has an admirer

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is shocked by the unexpected arrival of his ex-wife Maggie (Julie Graham), who appears to have forgotten that they separated years ago and wants to know why he left her. Eleanor goes on a date with local fisherman Paul Hale, an old friend she has not seen since school, while Martin and Louisa finally agree on a name for their baby - James Henry. With Martin Clunes, John Marquez and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Called to the home of an elderly couple who haven't been seen for a while, Janet discovers Eunice Bevan's severed head at the bottom of the stairs, her body at the top, and her husband Joe in bed, emaciated and barely able to speak. Meanwhile, the detective puts her house on the market, admitting her relationship with Ade is over, and three months in to Rachel's marriage to traffic cop Sean, she's starting to feel a little claustrophobic. George Costigan and Nicola Walker guest star, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Joyce tries to persuade Vera to take on Judy as a barmaid. Don lays down the law when Kevin questions his authority. Liz, Deirdre, Audrey, Alma and Maureen have a girls' night in

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vicky is furious when Steve patronises her. The men go to see Betty's Hot Shot race at Thirsk. Maureen is stunned to see a for sale sign up outside Reg's flat

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Dave's funeral arrives. Zak attempts to win back Lisa's favour. Kathy attacks Kim when she turns up at the funeral

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Kim she is not wanted at the funeral. Marlon and Butch vie for Sophie's attention. Roy tries to smash up Kim's car and blames her for killing his brother

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton organises a search of the moors after a farming couple are reported missing, leaving Jenny and Liz to look after their bemused children. Meanwhile, a reluctant Bernie is taken on a mini break to a country hotel by Vernon - but he grows increasingly dismayed over the plans for a new garage. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby asks Philip for a native charm to help him seduce the delectable Ruth - but his dreams of a romantic liaison soon go up in smoke. Comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens tell Rachel about their financial problems. Vic invites Viv to dinner to discuss their children. Steve finally tells Rachel how he really feels about her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic appears very tolerant of the idea of Donna moving in with Viv and Terry. Chris offers to buy Kim out of the stables and the golf course. Jack is paid a visit by Andy's teacher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve thinks the police are using Vicky to get to him. Jim appears in court. Vicky turns to Alec for help, telling him everything. Maureen claims Reg's flat as her marital home.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve visits Malcolm Fox to find out what is going on. Maureen has the locks changed on Reg's flat. Ashley and Kelly plan to spend the night together when Ken goes away on a course

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly's sister arrives in Aidensfield with a renowned Polish violinist and his daughter, intending to stage a concert at the village hall. Disaster strikes when an expensive instrument is stolen - and romance blossoms for Steve as he steps in to investigate the theft. Meanwhile, a shady father-and-son duo set their scheming sights on Vernon. Drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor is found dead on Halloween with a stake through her heart and Hobson reveals she shared a house with the victim in her university days. A medium visits Lewis claiming to have foreseen the tragedy, and ghostly messages spelled out in fridge magnet letters start appearing in a house occupied by four students. As the detective struggles with the possibility his friend is somehow implicated in the crime, a tale of jealousy and revenge begins to unfold. Rupert Graves (Sherlock) and Niamh Cusack (Heartbeat) guest star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: A gangland killing takes a surprising twist when it turns out the victim has been sexually mutilated. Andy punishes Janet by keeping her out of the heart of the investigation, and when she rebels, the ensuing row becomes personal. Rachel feels ambushed by Sean, who announces his mother wants to meet her - especially as she has spent years trying not to think about her own mum. Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Sean Maguire and Nicholas Gleaves star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet
